title: Beehiiv vs Flodesk (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and Flodesk email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your needs.
h1: Beehiiv vs Flodesk: Detailed Comparison for Email Marketing
intro: Both Beehiiv and Flodesk are modern email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and priorities. Beehiiv focuses on newsletter monetization and growth tools, while Flodesk emphasizes design and visual storytelling. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right platform for your email strategy.
choose_a_if: You want to monetize your newsletter through ads and sponsorships
You're starting out and need a free tier to test the platform
You prioritize growth analytics and audience segmentation tools
You're running a media publication or newsletter business
choose_b_if: Design and visual appeal are critical to your brand
You need unlimited subscribers without tier limitations
You want built-in e-commerce functionality like checkout forms
You're a visual creator, designer, or photographer
You prefer straightforward flat-rate pricing with no surprises

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—it depends on your priorities. Beehiiv excels at newsletter monetization and growth metrics, making it ideal for publishers. Flodesk wins on design quality and ease of use, making it better for visual brands and small e-commerce businesses. Compare your specific needs against their core strengths.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or Flodesk?
faq.a: Beehiiv is cheaper if you're just starting out, thanks to its free tier supporting up to 2,500 subscribers. Once you need paid plans, both start around $38-$39/month, but Beehiiv's pricing scales with subscriber count while Flodesk offers unlimited subscribers at flat rates, making Flodesk more cost-effective at scale.

faq.q: Can I switch from Flodesk to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, most email platforms support list imports and migrations. Both Beehiiv and Flodesk allow you to export your subscriber lists, though you may need to manually reconfigure workflows and design elements. Check with their support teams for specific migration guidance to ensure a smooth transition.
